The width of the Yuvaraja's houses should be 80, 74, 68, 62
and 56 cubits respectively and their length exceeds the breadth by
a third. The dimensions of the houses of the younger princes should
be half those of the Yuvaraja's,
[The length of these five varieties would be : 106-, 98-1, 90-3-,
82|- and 74|- cubits in order. The breadth and length of the princes'
houses would be 40 and 63^ cubits ; 37 and 494 ' ^4 and 454 > 31
and 414 ; and lastly 28 and 374 cubits respectively.]
\\*\\
The dimensions of the houses of feudatory chiefs, nobles and
king's officers would be equal to the difference between the measure-
